title: Lesson Quiz: Writing a Resume
description: Assessment of the concept that Drafting and organizing a resume.

choices.text: An early version of a resume that can be improved
choices.feedback: Correct. A draft is an early version that can be reviewed and revised.
A resume draft is not a legal contract.

choices.text: A casual message about a job
choices.feedback: A resume draft is a structured professional document.

choices.text: A list of random personal opinions
choices.feedback: A resume draft should contain relevant professional information, not random opinions.

questions.prompt: Relevant details are important when writing a resume.

choices.feedback: Relevant details matter because a resume should focus on information that supports its purpose.
Correct. A resume should focus on relevant details.

questions.prompt: Which detail would be most appropriate to include in a student resume draft?

choices.feedback: Favorite shows are not usually relevant resume content.
This is the right idea, but the strongest answer here is the one that more clearly emphasizes information supporting the resume purpose.

choices.text: Relevant skills, school information, and useful activities or experience
choices.feedback: Correct. These are the kinds of details that belong in a student resume draft.
Unrelated opinions do not belong in a resume.

questions.prompt: Why should a student revise a resume draft?

choices.text: To make the resume less organized
choices.feedback: Revision should improve organization, not weaken it.

choices.text: To remove all skills and experience
choices.feedback: Revision should strengthen relevant content, not remove useful information.

choices.text: To improve clarity, organization, and relevance before using the document
choices.feedback: This is close, but the strongest answer here is the one that most directly emphasizes improving the draft before submission or use.

choices.text: To improve the draft so the information is clearer and more professional
choices.feedback: Correct. Revision helps make a resume clearer, more relevant, and more professional.

questions.prompt: A resume should include information that supports its purpose.

choices.feedback: Correct. Resume content should support the purpose of the document.
Resumes should focus on relevant information that supports the goal of the document.
